front: What is IT Monitoring in capacity planning?
back: Systematic observation and measurement of system components, applications, and network resources to ensure optimal performance and availability, providing data for capacity planning decisions.

front: Name the three main types of monitoring
back: 1) System Monitoring (CPU, memory, storage) 2) Network Monitoring (bandwidth, latency, packet loss) 3) Application Monitoring (response time, errors, transactions)

front: What are Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in monitoring?
back: Quantifiable measures used to evaluate success in meeting objectives. Examples: CPU utilization >80%, Memory usage <90%, Response time <2s, Uptime >99.9%.
A monitoring approach where alerts are triggered when a metric exceeds or falls below predefined values (thresholds), enabling proactive incident management.

front: What is the difference between active and passive monitoring?
back: Active monitoring sends test transactions to measure response times (synthetic monitoring). Passive monitoring captures real traffic and events without injection.
A passive monitoring technique that captures actual user interactions with applications, collecting data on page load times, clicks, and user behavior in real-world scenarios.
Active monitoring using automated scripts to simulate user transactions at regular intervals, testing application performance without real users.

front: Define MTTR in capacity planning context
back: Mean Time To Repair - Average time taken to restore a failed system to operational status. Lower MTTR indicates better incident response capability.
Process of establishing normal performance patterns over time under typical conditions, used as reference for detecting anomalies and planning capacity.
Percentage of available capacity currently in use. Formula: (Used Resources / Total Resources) x 100. Critical for capacity planning forecasts.

front: What are the five golden signals in monitoring?
back: 1) Latency 2) Traffic 3) Errors 4) Saturation 5) Availability. These core metrics help assess system health.
Application Performance Monitoring - Specialized monitoring for tracking application transactions, response times, error rates, and user experience metrics.

front: What is the purpose of capacity trending?
back: Analyzing historical data to predict future resource needs, identifying growth patterns, and planning infrastructure upgrades before bottlenecks occur.

front: What is log aggregation in monitoring?
back: Centralized collection of logs from multiple sources for analysis, troubleshooting, and identifying patterns across the IT infrastructure.
A quantitative measure of some aspect of the level of service provided (e.g., request latency, error rate). Forms the basis for SLOs and SLAs.
